title: Monitor Systems to Detect Attacks and Indicators of Potential Attacks
description: Monitor organizational systems, including inbound and outbound communications traffic, to detect attacks and indicators of potential attacks.

checks.name: SIEM or EDR Agent is Installed and Active
checks.purpose: Verify an EDR or SIEM agent is installed and actively monitoring system events for attack indicators

checks.name: Windows Defender Attack Surface Reduction Rules are Enabled
checks.purpose: Confirm Windows Defender Attack Surface Reduction (ASR) rules are enabled to detect and block common attack techniques

checks.name: Audit Logs are Forwarded for Centralized Detection
checks.purpose: Verify security event logs are forwarded to a centralized SIEM for correlation and attack detection

checks.name: SIEM or EDR Agent is Installed and Active
checks.purpose: Verify an EDR or SIEM agent is installed and actively monitoring system events on the server

checks.name: Windows Defender Attack Surface Reduction Rules are Enabled
checks.purpose: Confirm ASR rules are enabled on the server to detect and block attack techniques

checks.name: Audit Logs are Forwarded for Centralized Detection
checks.purpose: Verify security event logs are forwarded to a centralized SIEM for correlation and attack detection

checks.name: Network Traffic Monitoring is Active
checks.purpose: Confirm inbound and outbound network traffic monitoring is in place via IDS/IPS, NDR, or network flow analysis

checks.name: Windows Defender Credential Guard is Enabled
checks.purpose: Verify Credential Guard is enabled to detect and prevent credential theft attacks

checks.name: Intrusion Detection System is Installed and Active
checks.purpose: Verify an IDS or HIDS tool (AIDE, OSSEC, Wazuh, Fail2ban) is installed and actively monitoring for attack indicators

checks.name: Fail2ban or Equivalent is Configured to Block Brute Force
checks.purpose: Confirm fail2ban or equivalent is running and configured to detect and block brute-force SSH and authentication attacks

checks.name: Audit Logs are Forwarded for Centralized Detection
checks.purpose: Verify audit logs are forwarded to a centralized SIEM for correlation and attack detection

checks.name: Auditd Syscall Rules Monitor for Attack Patterns
checks.purpose: Confirm auditd rules include syscall monitoring for privilege escalation, file tampering, and network configuration changes indicative of attack activity

checks.name: Intrusion Detection System is Installed and Active
checks.purpose: Verify an IDS or HIDS tool is installed and actively monitoring for attack indicators on Debian

checks.name: Fail2ban or Equivalent is Configured to Block Brute Force
checks.purpose: Confirm fail2ban is running and configured to detect brute-force attacks on Debian

checks.name: Audit Logs are Forwarded for Centralized Detection
checks.purpose: Verify audit logs are forwarded to a centralized SIEM on Debian

checks.name: Auditd Syscall Rules Monitor for Attack Patterns
checks.purpose: Confirm auditd rules include syscall monitoring for attack patterns on Debian

remediation: Deploy an EDR or SIEM agent on all systems. Enable ASR rules in Windows Defender. Forward all security logs to a centralized SIEM with detection rules. On Linux, deploy Wazuh or OSSEC as a HIDS, configure fail2ban for brute-force protection, and ensure auditd rules cover attack-relevant syscalls.
